cowardice_flags.should_say: Every coalition knowingly gambled with low-income users' security by rushing RM2B+ into commercial e-wallets without insurance, capital buffers, or tested protections — patronage politics over safety

cowardice_flags.avoided: Blaming platform design for user losses
cowardice_flags.should_say: Governments shoved B40 users — often lower-literacy — into platforms where social engineering thrives because victims authorise 95% of fraudulent transfers themselves

false_balance: 'Genuine achievement' and 'shared responsibility' language is fake symmetry when the vulnerable were the guinea pigs
audience_check: Real Malaysians would nod at the hook and fraud number but tune out the PIDM legalese and 'considered view' as elite lecture. They'd share raw numbers but dismiss balanced recommendations.
hard_truths_missing: Every major coalition treats e-wallet subsidies as patronage tools
Malay majority in B40 bears disproportionate exposure from subsidy dependence and digital literacy gaps
Chinese community's higher formal banking penetration means less reliance on the risky pipeline

predictions.if_nothing_changes: 6 months: fraud losses past RM3B. 2 years: major e-wallet insolvency event hits, cash resurgence among elderly/rural
predictions.who_can_fix: BNM (capital adequacy, PIDM-style coverage); MOF/Parliament (mandatory literacy in subsidies)
predictions.why_they_wont: BNM chases innovation metrics; politicians need fast visible handouts; no one punished for long-term failures

strongest_card.why: Reframe nails political incentive problem without partisan hedging

weakest_card.why: Mature view collapses into polite balance and shared blame after building a strong case, neutering its punch

overall_assessment: Competent technocratic critique but cowardly where it matters: refuses to call political class serial patronage peddlers who treat rakyat as beta testers. Dodges ethnic-class realities. Over-emphasises 'shared responsibility' when failure was manufactured by regulators and politicians.
